intro: A snapshot of where I am right now, what I am practicing, and the bits of life snapshot.

sections.title: Where I Am
items: I just received a contractor offer as a software engineer in Tokyo, so I ended my working-holiday life in Australia and Japan earlier than expected.
I want to build my own life in Tokyo: favorite shops, favorite corners, and favorite people.

sections.title: Recent Practices
items: Moving from pessimism toward optimism
Not getting anxious too early
Finding my exercise habit again
Raising my Japanese from everyday conversation to business level
Trying to design my life with Be Your Own Life Designer. I once used the idea of Atomic Habits to draw my own map.
Studying system design with Modern System Design Bootcamp
Trying to keep up with AI. Recently I have been thinking about Harnesses Engineering.

sections.title: Recent Achievements
items: Found a software engineering job in Tokyo as a contractor
Made quite a few friends in English at my share house
Got better at cooking for myself

sections.title: Recent Frustrations
items: Japanese
The regular routine I had during working holiday has fallen apart again
How to choose, trade off, and let go

sections.title: Recent Discoveries
items: The world is truly huge. Huge enough that I can always find people somewhere doing things similar to what I am doing. No one can really be that special, but it also means that even when I feel out of place, there must be a corner of the world that can hold me.

Having a Western face does not automatically mean someone is good at English. I have met people from Latin America and Europe whose English is also shaky. Even blond, blue-eyed people can get nervous speaking English like I do, though many of them are brave enough to ask questions and clarify things.

sections.title: Recent Favorites
items: J-pop: Airi Suzuki, milet, Ryokuoushoku Shakai
Manga: Fujii on the Roadside, 100 Meters
Book: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
Movie: Emergency Declaration
